props.content: Analysis of 2.5M+ rides across 29 regions from Nov 25, 2025 to Feb 21, 2026 reveals strong LyftMaps adoption (97.1%) with stable compliance around 90.9%. New York City leads in volume (183K rides), while Boston achieves highest compliance (92.8%). Reroute costs show a declining trend from $0.48 to $0.45 per ride, indicating improving navigation efficiency.

props.title: Compliance vs LyftMaps Adoption by Region

props.alt: Correlation heatmap showing relationships between key metrics
props.caption: Moderate negative correlation between compliance and reroute cost (-0.34)
Distribution analysis of compliance, LyftMaps adoption, and reroute costs

props.title: Strong LyftMaps Dominance
props.content: LyftMaps captures 97.1% of navigation usage, with Waze at 1.9% and Google Maps at 0.9%. This near-monopoly position provides strong control over routing decisions.

props.title: Regional Compliance Variance
props.content: Compliance ranges from 88.4% (Newark) to 92.8% (Boston), a 4.4 percentage point spread. Newark, Las Vegas, and Houston show the lowest compliance rates and may require targeted interventions.

props.title: Declining Reroute Costs
props.content: Reroute costs peaked at $0.49 in late December and declined to $0.45 by late February, suggesting improving navigation efficiency or route optimization over time.

props.title: Weak Correlation Patterns
props.content: Compliance shows weak negative correlation with LyftMaps adoption (-0.12) and moderate negative correlation with reroute cost (-0.34). Higher compliance regions tend to have slightly lower reroute costs, but the relationship is not strong.
